feed.title: Evan Lin's L1fe
feed.subtitle: A minimal, responsive and feature-rich Jekyll theme for technical writing.

entry.title: Play with me t_t
entry.summary: June 3, 2025 Today after work I suddenly felt like playing some Minecraft. It’s been a while since I last logged in, and for some reason the idea just popped into my head while I was on the way home. Playing alone can get a bit boring sometimes, so I messaged 2hi5hu and asked if he wanted to join later in the evening. At first he said “maybe”, which I took as a yes. I spent a little time prep...

entry.title: Happy birthday
entry.summary: Nov 23, 2024 Today is my birthday. I didn’t really plan to celebrate it in any special way—just another normal day. When I woke up this morning, I noticed a few messages from friends wishing me a happy birthday. It was a small thing, but it definitely made the morning feel a bit warmer. Work was pretty normal. A couple of coworkers found out it was my birthday and insisted on getting lunch to...

entry.title: How they found me??
entry.summary: September 2, 2024 Something a little weird happened today and it’s been bothering me for most of the evening. Recently I started noticing a few random people mentioning one of my old usernames in messages and comments. It’s not a name I’ve used for a long time, so seeing it suddenly appear again felt pretty strange. I honestly can’t remember where it might have been exposed. What surprised m...

entry.title: Don't spam
entry.summary: August 9, 2024 Something a bit strange happened today. When I checked my inbox this morning, I noticed a bunch of emails from people I didn’t recognize. Most of them looked like random messages or advertisements, and a few were clearly spam. At first I thought it was just the usual junk mail that slips through filters, but the number of messages kept increasing throughout the day. It made me ...

entry.title: Normal life
entry.summary: July 6, 2024 Work was actually pretty interesting today. In the afternoon I spent some time discussing a small technical problem with my colleague shukuang. I’ve always felt that he’s one of those people who can understand complicated systems incredibly quickly. We were looking at a strange bug that had been bothering me for a while. I had already tried a few different approaches but couldn’t...
